steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank fills the subject of “found” and consists of a title (US biologist) directly followed by a proper name (Brad Gemmell), then “and colleagues.” The name identifies which biologist is being referenced.

steps.title: Apply appositive punctuation rules
steps.content: A restrictive appositive—one that provides essential information to identify a noun—should not be set off with commas. Here, Brad Gemmell is restrictive; it specifies which US biologist, so no comma separates the title from the name. Choices A and C incorrectly treat the name as a nonrestrictive appositive requiring commas on both sides. Choice D inconsistently places a comma only before “and colleagues” without matching punctuation around the name. Choice B correctly uses no punctuation between the title and name. That matches choice B — B is correct.
